selected_important_turn.text: I can give you 3 wood packages if I can get 3 water and 2 food. So you will receive 3 wood and 1 food. What do you think?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is a high-leverage turning point: after cooperative information-sharing and the opponent indicating needs, you switch to a firm, self-favoring allocation that claims almost all of the water and food while giving the opponent all wood. That move reframes the interaction back toward zero-sum competition and strongly affects whether a fair, easy agreement will be reached.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It's too greedy and rigid given the prior cooperative exchange and the opponent's stated preferences. It ignores the opponent's explicit needs (they asked for 2 wood and prefer water over food) and doesn't justify the ask or propose tradeoffs beyond a hard take-it-or-leave-it. That increases risk of rejection or resentment and reduces integrative potential.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften and turn this into a tentative, principled trade: present a smaller, justifiable request or a conditional package (e.g., ask for fewer water/food, explain why you need them, or propose a logroll such as giving up some wood in exchange for a modest amount of water). Offer it as a proposal open to revision and invite their counter-options rather than a maximal demand.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (if softened — opens room for mutually beneficial adjustments; as-is it risks lowering joint gains)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (a more balanced ask would be perceived as fairer)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (less resistance and fewer rejections if framed cooperatively)

value2reason.Low: Firewood would prefer warmth and it is important for my family.
value2reason.Medium: We would want extra food just in case anything goes bad or if someone gets sick.
value2reason.High: My family drinks a lot of water and I'm worried that we'll run out.

value2reason.Low: I think food is important. Food tastes good and keeps you happy.
value2reason.Medium: Because I might be thirsty and need water.  Water is needed to keep you hydrated.
value2reason.High: Because I would want to keep warm.

dialogue.text: On my upcoming trip, I would like some extra food and water for my family.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ok. I would like to have some extra firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Do you have a preference between food and water?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I would like water more than food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: How many wood do you think you need?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I would like 2 of the firewood. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I don't need wood as I am usually warm so I think we can work out a deal. I like to have more water and I usually get thirsty.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds good to me. 🙂 I think we can make a good deal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can give you 3 wood packages if I can get 3 water and 2 food. So you will receive 3 wood and 1 food. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think 3 wood and 1 food is fair, but I’m willing to adjust if you’re okay with 2 wood and 2 food instead. It would balance our needs better.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with 2 wood and 2 food. Let’s finalize this deal and split the rest as we go.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think 2 wood and 2 food is fair. Let’s split the remaining 3 water and 1 food between us. How does that sound?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think we can split the remaining 3 water and 1 food evenly. Let’s agree on 2 water and 1 food for me, and 1 water and 0 food for you. That should work for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that split works for me. Let me confirm: you get 2 wood, 2 food, 1 water, and I get 1 wood, 1 food, 2 water. That covers all packages, right?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Let me just confirm: I get 2 wood, 2 food, 1 water, and you get 1 wood, 1 food, 2 water. That covers all packages, right?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds fair. Let's agree on this split and head out. Have a great trip!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Agreed on the split. Have a great trip!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
